original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Society for Reproductive Medicine Practice Committee
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Current evaluation of amenorrhea: a committee opinion (2024)
original_registry_entry.population: Postmenarchal adolescents and adults presenting with primary or secondary amenorrhea, with pregnancy, age, pubertal, endocrine and anatomic qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for recorded menstrual, pregnancy, medication, endocrine, examination and clinician-selected investigation context. It does not diagnose a cause, select treatment, or define UAE referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Introduction, definitions and clinical history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing primary and secondary amenorrhea and recording menarche, cycle, pregnancy possibility, weight, exercise, medicines, galactorrhea, androgenic and vasomotor symptoms without assigning a cause.

exact_sections.heading: Physical examination and initial laboratory evaluation
exact_sections.locator: official HTML examination and laboratory-evaluation sections
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed pubertal, androgenic, thyroid and pelvic context and clinician-selected pregnancy, TSH, prolactin and FSH review; it does not auto-order or interpret tests.

exact_sections.heading: Cause-directed evaluation and imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-directed pelvic imaging and additional evaluation only after history, examination and initial results narrow the context.

title: Current evaluation of amenorrhea: a committee opinion (2024)
publisher: American Society for Reproductive Medicine Practice Committee
population: Postmenarchal adolescents and adults presenting with primary or secondary amenorrhea, with pregnancy, age, pubertal, endocrine and anatomic qualifiers.
